description: Automatically extract and update SHALLOW/DEEP keywords in the depth classifier based on real MongoDB conversation history. Use this skill whenever you need to keep the classifier aligned with actual user interaction patterns, after deploying new features, when accuracy drops, or during regular maintenance.

evals.name: extract-and-update-classifier
evals.prompt: Update the depth classifier for the Kazusa AI chatbot. Extract keywords from the MongoDB conversation_history collection (target 500 recent messages from the last 2 weeks). Classify each message as SHALLOW or DEEP, extract 3-6 word phrases, deduplicate, and ensure 45-55% English/Chinese balance. Then automatically update src/kazusa_ai_chatbot/rag/depth_classifier.py with the new SHALLOW_KEYWORDS and DEEP_KEYWORDS lists. Run the classifier tests to verify accuracy is >85%.
evals.expected_output: Updated depth_classifier.py file with new keyword lists, test output showing accuracy >85%, a summary showing sample size, language balance, and keyword counts for both categories

assertions.name: classifier-file-updated
assertions.description: The SHALLOW_KEYWORDS and DEEP_KEYWORDS lists in depth_classifier.py have been updated with new keywords extracted from conversation history

assertions.name: language-balance
assertions.description: The updated keyword lists maintain 40-60% English/Chinese balance (not heavily skewed to one language)

assertions.name: keyword-counts
assertions.description: SHALLOW_KEYWORDS list has 25-45 keywords, DEEP_KEYWORDS list has 30-50 keywords (reasonable sizes for effective classification)

assertions.name: test-accuracy
assertions.description: Running the classifier tests produces accuracy ≥85% and all test assertions pass

assertions.name: executable-script
assertions.description: The update_classifier.py script is callable with proper error handling and logging output

evals.name: dry-run-extraction
evals.prompt: Run the depth classifier updater in dry-run mode (--output-only flag) without updating the actual classifier file. Extract and analyze keywords from 300 recent MongoDB messages, classify them, and output the proposed SHALLOW and DEEP keyword lists. Show the frequency counts for each keyword. Do NOT update the classifier file itself.
evals.expected_output: Console output showing extracted keywords with frequency counts, language distribution, and proposed keyword lists for both SHALLOW and DEEP categories (no file modifications)

assertions.name: no-file-modifications
assertions.description: The depth_classifier.py file remains unchanged (--output-only flag prevents file updates)

assertions.name: output-shows-keywords
assertions.description: Output displays the proposed keyword lists including frequency counts and language breakdown

assertions.name: sample-size-shown
assertions.description: Output indicates how many messages were sampled and analyzed

assertions.name: language-distribution-visible
assertions.description: Output clearly shows percentage split between English and Chinese keywords

evals.name: custom-sample-size
evals.prompt: Update the depth classifier with a custom sample size of 800 messages (using --sample-size 800 flag). Extract keywords from the last 2 weeks of conversation history, perform full classification and deduplication, maintain language balance, update the classifier file, and verify tests pass. The larger sample should produce more stable and representative keyword lists.
evals.expected_output: Updated depth_classifier.py with keywords extracted from 800-message sample, test results showing accuracy >85%, summary showing achieved sample size and keyword distribution

assertions.name: sample-size-honored
assertions.description: The script respected the --sample-size 800 parameter and extracted approximately 800 messages

assertions.name: classifier-updated
assertions.description: The SHALLOW_KEYWORDS and DEEP_KEYWORDS lists have been updated in the classifier file

assertions.name: test-results
assertions.description: Running classifier tests shows >85% accuracy with the updated keywords

assertions.name: larger-sample-coverage
assertions.description: Larger sample size results in 40+ SHALLOW keywords and 45+ DEEP keywords (more comprehensive coverage)
